compositional_truth_check.findings: P3 (Pothos) is present with the rabbit anchor. P1 (String of Nickels) is present in the yellow pot. P2/P4 are not visible as distinct specimens; the black pot appears to contain only a single, struggling Pothos cutting/vine.

systemic_losses: P2 (Mexican Mint) and P4 (Silver Guest) are not identifiable in the black pot. The soil surface is devoid of these specimens.
new_introductions: Electronic component (IC/chip) introduced to the desk surface at T-3.
Eggshell fragments used as soil top-dressing in the Pothos pot.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Appears dormant or empty. No visible foliage above the soil line.
plant_audit.P2_P4: Systemic Loss: No evidence of Mexican Mint or Silver Guest.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: Single vine with one pale leaf. Shows signs of chlorosis and extreme stress.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp appearance. No fungal blooms, but significant debris (eggshells) present.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Cluttered with non-botanical items (receipt, electronic component).

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Introduction of external debris (receipt, electronic component).
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_Current: The Pothos vine remains static; no new growth or recovery observed.

anomalies: Presence of electronic components on the desk surface.
Use of eggshell fragments as a soil amendment/top-dressing.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of severe decline. The Pothos (P3) is the only surviving specimen, exhibiting extreme chlorosis and lack of vigor. The other registered plants (P1, P2, P4) are either missing or have suffered total systemic loss. The environment is cluttered with non-botanical debris, and the lack of visible growth suggests a failure in the current maintenance protocol.
